qas.q: who is required to have a driver's license?
qas.a: anyone who operates a motor vehicle or motor driven cycle on public roadways in Missouri.
Eligible: 15 for young drivers, allows a young person to learn to drive with the supervision of a licensed adult.
Eligible: 15 1/2 or 16 you may apply to learn to operate a motorcycle or motortricylce .
Eliglible :16 Second step in Missouri's graduated driver license program for young drivers, this allows a young driver certain restricted driving privileges.
Eligible :18 basic driver's license; operator license, must have a class f license to operate any motor vehicle, does not allow you to drive a motorcycle unless the license shows the M endorsement.
Eligible :16 need to pass the class F and Class m written, vision,road sign, and motorcycle skills tests.
based on the type of commercial motor vehicle you drive. Must be at least 18 years old to apply for a CDL, and at least 21 years old to obtain a CDL with a hazmat or school bus endorsement.
Photo non driver license for identification purposes, expires on your date of birth in the sixth year after you apply. If you are age 70 or older , your non-driver license will never expire.

qas.q: Requirements to Obtain A license or Permit
qas.a: verification of name, date of birth, place of birth, social security number, and Missouri residential address.

Pass the vision, road sign, and written tests. A parent must accompany you.

under the age of 16, you may drive only when accompanied in the front seat by a parent, stepparent, grand parent or legal guardian is physically disabled, he or she may designate up to two persons to accompany you in the vehicle to provide behind the wheel instruction.
you may drive only when accompanied in the front seat by a person who is at least 21 years old and has a valid driver's license.
